  set M := max (|a|) (|b|) with hM_def

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_06 {f : ℝ × ℝ → ℝ}
    (hpos : ∀ x y : ℝ, y > 0 → f (x, y) = Real.sqrt (x ^ 2 + y ^ 2))
    (hzero : ∀ x : ℝ, f (x, 0) = x)
    (hneg : ∀ x y : ℝ, y < 0 → f (x, y) = -Real.sqrt (x ^ 2 + y ^ 2)) :
    ¬ DifferentiableAt ℝ f (0, 0) := by
  intro hdiff
  obtain ⟨Df, hDf⟩ := hdiff
  -- Directional derivative along (1,1): limit of f(t,t)/t as t→0
  -- For t>0: f(t,t)=√(2t²)=t√2, limit=√2
  -- For t<0: f(t,t)=-√(2t²)=t√2, limit=√2
  -- If differentiable, Df(1,1) would be Df(1,0)+Df(0,1)=1+1=2 ≠ √2, contradiction
  have h_dir_11 : Df (1, 1) = Real.sqrt 2 := by
    -- compute from definition of directional derivative
    sorry
  have h_linear : Df (1, 1) = Df (1, 0) + Df (0, 1) := by
    simpa using Df.map_add (1, 0) (0, 1)
  have h_df_10 : Df (1, 0) = 1 := by
    -- from f(t,0) = t
    sorry
  have h_df_01 : Df (0, 1) = 1 := by
    -- from f(0,t) = t for all t
    sorry
  rw [h_df_10, h_df_01] at h_linear
  have h_sqrt_two_ne_two : Real.sqrt 2 ≠ (2 : ℝ) := by
    nlinarith [show (Real.sqrt 2)^2 = 2 from Real.pow_sqrt_eq_abs 2 |>.trans (by norm_num)]
  rw [h_dir_11] at h_linear
  nlinarith

ma-proofbench-0019:
import Mathlib

open Filter Topology

noncomputable def xSeq (a : ℕ → ℝ) : ℕ → ℝ :=
  λ n => ∑ k in Finset.range (n + 1), (a k + 1 / a k)

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_19 {a : ℕ → ℝ} (ha : ∀ n, 0 < a n) :
    Tendsto (fun n => 1 / xSeq a n) atTop (𝓝 0) := by
  -- By AM-GM: a_k + 1/a_k ≥ 2 for all k (since (√a_k - 1/√a_k)² ≥ 0)
  -- Hence xSeq a n ≥ 2(n+1) → ∞, so 1/xSeq a n ≤ 1/(2(n+1)) → 0
  have h_amgm : ∀ k, 2 ≤ a k + 1 / a k := by
    intro k
    have hpos := ha k
    have h_sq : (Real.sqrt (a k) - 1 / Real.sqrt (a k)) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    nlinarith [Real.sq_sqrt hpos.le]
  have h_lb : ∀ n, 2 * ((n : ℝ) + 1) ≤ xSeq a n := by
    intro n
    dsimp [xSeq]
    calc
      2 * ((n : ℝ) + 1) = ∑ k in Finset.range (n + 1), (2 : ℝ) := by simp [Finset.sum_range_succ]
      _ ≤ ∑ k in Finset.range (n + 1), (a k + 1 / a k) := Finset.sum_le_sum (λ k _ => h_amgm k)
      _ = xSeq a n := rfl
  have h_pos : ∀ n, 0 < xSeq a n := by
    intro n; linarith [h_lb n]
  apply tendsto_of_tendsto_of_tendsto_of_le_of_le (by simp) ?_ ?_
  · refine eventually_of_forall (λ n => by positivity)
  · refine eventually_of_forall (λ n => ?_)
    refine (one_div_le_one_div (h_pos n) (by nlinarith [h_lb n])).mpr (h_lb n)
    -- 1/x ≤ 1/(2(n+1)) since 2(n+1) ≤ x
ma-proofbench-0020:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_19_b (n : ℕ) (hnpos : 0 < n) (hnodd : Odd n) :
    ∃! x : ℝ, (∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), x ^ k / (Nat.factorial k)) = 0 := by
  set f := λ x : ℝ => ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), x ^ k / (Nat.factorial k : ℝ) with hf
  -- f is the (n+1)-th Taylor polynomial of e^x
  -- For odd n: f(-∞) = -∞, f(0) = 1, f'(x) > 0 for all x (since (n-1) is even)
  -- Thus f is strictly increasing and has exactly one real zero
  have h_f0 : f 0 = 1 := by simp [hf]
  have h_deriv_pos : ∀ x, 0 < deriv f x := by
    -- deriv f = f_{n-1}(x) = truncated exponential of even degree, always positive
    sorry
  have h_lim_neg : Tendsto f atBot (𝓝 (-∞ : ℝ)) := by
    -- Leading term x^n/n! with odd n dominates as x → -∞
    sorry
  have h_strictMono : StrictMono f := strictMono_of_deriv_pos h_deriv_pos (by
    -- f is C^1
    refine ContDiff.differentiable ?_ le_rfl
    refine contDiff_finset_sum _ (λ k _ => ?_)
    refine (contDiff_id.pow _).div_const _)
  -- By IVT and strict monotonicity, there is a unique zero
  obtain ⟨x₀, hx₀⟩ := intermediate_value_Ioo' h_lim_neg (by
    filter_upwards [h_lim_neg] with x hx; exact hx) h_f0 (Set.mem_Ioi.mpr (0 : ℝ))
  refine ⟨x₀, hx₀, λ y hy => ?_⟩
  exact h_strictMono.eq_iff_eq.mp (by rw [hx₀, hy])

import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_21 {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
    {a : ℝ} (ha : 0 < a) {f : ℝ → E} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc (-a) a))
    (hodd : Function.Odd f) : (∫ x in (-a)..a, f x) = 0 := by
  have h_int := hf.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by linarith)
  calc
    (∫ x in (-a)..a, f x) = (∫ x in (-a)..(0 : ℝ), f x) + (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..a, f x) :=
      integral_add_adjacent_intervals
        (hf.mono (Set.Icc_subset_Icc_right (by linarith))).intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by linarith)
        (hf.mono (Set.Icc_subset_Icc_left (by linarith))).intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by linarith)
    _ = (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..a, f (-x)) + (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..a, f x) := by
      rw [integral_comp_neg (h_int.restrict _)]; simp
    _ = (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..a, -f x) + (∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..a, f x) := by
      refine congrArg (· + _) (integral_congr (λ x hx => ?_))
      rw [hodd (-x)]; simp
    _ = 0 := by ring
ma-proofbench-0024:
import Mathlib

open Function

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_23 (u : ℕ → ℝ) :
    (∃ φ : ℕ → ℕ, StrictMono φ ∧ Monotone (u ∘ φ)) ∨
    (∃ φ : ℕ → ℕ, StrictMono φ ∧ Antitone (u ∘ φ)) := by
  -- Classic result: every sequence has a monotone subsequence
  -- A "peak" n satisfies u_n ≥ u_m for all m > n
  -- Case 1: infinitely many peaks → decreasing subsequence
  -- Case 2: finitely many peaks → increasing subsequence after last peak
  by_cases h : ∀ N, ∃ n ≥ N, ∀ m > n, u n ≥ u m
  · -- Infinitely many peaks: select them in order
    left
    -- Build φ recursively: φ(0) is any peak, φ(k+1) is a peak > φ(k)
    sorry
  · -- Only finitely many peaks: after the last peak, build increasing subsequence
    push_neg at h
    rcases h with ⟨N, hN⟩
    right
    -- For each n ≥ N, since n is not a peak, ∃ m > n with u_m > u_n
    -- Use this to build a strictly increasing subsequence
    sorry
ma-proofbench-0029:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_28 : ¬ ∃ f : ℝ → ℝ, Continuous f ∧
    (∀ᵐ x ∂(MeasureTheory.volume), f x = Set.indicator (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) (fun _ => (1 : ℝ)) x) := by
  rintro ⟨f, hf_cont, hf_ae⟩
  -- f equals χ_{[0,1]} almost everywhere
  -- Since f is continuous, its range is an interval (connected)
  -- But f only takes values 0 and 1 a.e., so by continuity f ≡ 0 or f ≡ 1
  -- If f ≡ 0, contradiction since f = 1 on [0,1] (measure 1)
  -- If f ≡ 1, contradiction since f = 0 on ℝ\[0,1] (infinite measure)
  have h_range : Set.range f ⊆ {0, 1} := by
    rintro y ⟨x, rfl⟩
    -- By continuity, f(x) ∈ ({0,1} minus measure-zero exceptions)
    -- If f(x) ∉ {0,1}, then by continuity f ≠ χ a.e. on some neighborhood → contradiction
    sorry
  have h_connected : IsConnected (Set.range f) :=
    hf_cont.continuousOn.isConnected_image (isConnected_univ)
  -- The only nonempty connected subsets of {0,1} are {0} and {1}
  have h_const : (∀ x, f x = 0) ∨ (∀ x, f x = 1) := by
    have h_nonempty : Set.Nonempty (Set.range f) := ⟨f 0, by simp⟩
    rcases Set.subset_connected_of_isConnected h_connected h_nonempty h_range with h
    -- h : range f = {0} or range f = {1}
    sorry
  rcases h_const with (h0 | h1)
  · -- f ≡ 0 but equals 1 on [0,1] a.e. Contradiction
    have h_measure : volume (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) > 0 := by rw [volume_Icc]; positivity
    sorry
  · -- f ≡ 1 but equals 0 outside [0,1] a.e. Contradiction  
    have h_measure : volume ((Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)ᶜ) = ∞ := by
      rw [Set.compl_Icc, volume_union]
      sorry
    sorry

theorem algebra_569189 (x : ℝ) : |Real.sqrt (x ^ 2 + x + 1) - Real.sqrt (x ^ 2 - x + 1)| < 1 := by
  have h_pos1 : 0 < x ^ 2 + x + 1 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg x]
  have h_pos2 : 0 < x ^ 2 - x + 1 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg x]
  by_cases hx : x = 0
  · subst hx; norm_num
  · -- Rationalize: |√A - √B| = |A-B|/(√A+√B) = |2x|/(√A+√B)
    -- Since √A > |x| and √B ≥ 0, the denominator > |x|
    -- So |√A - √B| = |2x|/(√A+√B) < |2x|/|x| = 2 (not enough)
    -- Need stronger: √A+√B > 2|x| to get < 1
    -- Actually: x²+x+1 = (x+1/2)²+3/4 > x²+1/4 > |x|²
    -- So √(x²+x+1) > |x| and √(x²-x+1) > |x|
    -- Thus denominator > 2|x|, giving the result
    sorry
formalmath-l3-0002:
import Mathlib

open Topology Filter

theorem DEMIMathAnalysis_26 :
    ¬ ∃ (f g h : ℝ → ℝ), Continuous f ∧ Continuous g ∧ Continuous h ∧ ∀ x y : ℝ, h (f x + g y) = x * y := by
  rintro ⟨f, g, h, hf, hg, hh, H⟩
  -- From H: h(f(x)+g(y)) = xy for all real x,y
  -- Set y=0: h(f(x)+g(0)) = 0 for all x → h≡0 on set A = {f(x)+g(0)}
  -- Set x=0: h(f(0)+g(y)) = 0 for all y → h≡0 on set B = {f(0)+g(y)}
  -- But H(1,1): h(f(1)+g(1)) = 1 ≠ 0
  -- A ∪ B is connected (they intersect at f(0)+g(0))
  -- So h takes both 0 and 1 on a connected set → by IVT, contradiction
  have hy0 : ∀ x, h (f x + g 0) = 0 := λ x => by simpa using H x 0
  have hx0 : ∀ y, h (f 0 + g y) = 0 := λ y => by simpa using H 0 y
  have h11 : h (f 1 + g 1) = 1 := by simpa using H 1 1
  -- The sets are connected; h being continuous would force f(1)+g(1) to belong to either A or B
  -- But then h would be 0 there, contradicting h=1
  sorry

import Mathlib

open Real

theorem olymid_ref_base_3533 {F : ℝ → ℝ} (hf0 : F 0 = -1) (hf' : Differentiable ℝ F)
    (hf'1 : ∀ x, deriv F x = sin (sin (sin (sin x))) * cos (sin (sin x)) * cos (sin x) * cos x) :
    F = fun x => -cos (sin (sin (sin x))) := by
  -- Recognize derivative as chain rule: d/dx[-cos(sin(sin(sin x)))] = sin(sin(sin(sin x)))·cos(sin(sin x))·cos(sin x)·cos x
  -- So F'(x) = G'(x) where G(x) = -cos(sin(sin(sin x)))
  -- Hence F = G + C. Since F(0) = -1 and G(0) = -cos(0) = -1, C = 0.
  set G := λ x : ℝ => -cos (sin (sin (sin x))) with hG
  have hG_deriv : ∀ x, deriv G x = sin (sin (sin (sin x))) * cos (sin (sin x)) * cos (sin x) * cos x := by
    intro x
    -- Compute derivative by repeated chain rule
    dsimp [G]
    simp [deriv_cos, deriv_sin, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
    ring
  have h_deriv_eq : deriv F = deriv G := by
    ext x; rw [hf'1 x, hG_deriv x]
  -- Two differentiable functions with equal derivatives differ by a constant
  have h_const : ∃ C : ℝ, F = λ x => G x + C := by
    refine (deriv_eq_zero_iff_const_sub ?_ ?_).mp ?_
    -- F - G has derivative 0 everywhere
    sorry
  rcases h_const with ⟨C, hC⟩
  have hC_zero : C = 0 := by
    have := congrFun hC 0
    rw [hf0] at this
    simp [hG, sin_zero] at this
    linarith
  subst hC_zero
  exact hC

import Mathlib

open Real Set

theorem algebra_53918 (x : ℝ) : cos x ≥ 1 - x ^ 2 / 2 := by
  -- Define g(x) = cos x - (1 - x²/2)
  -- g(0) = 0, g'(x) = -sin x + x, g''(x) = 1 - cos x ≥ 0
  -- g is convex, g'(0) = 0, so g(0) is global minimum → g(x) ≥ 0
  set g := λ t : ℝ => cos t - (1 - t ^ 2 / 2) with hg
  have hg0 : g 0 = 0 := by dsimp [g]; norm_num
  have hg'_zero : deriv g 0 = 0 := by
    dsimp [g]
    simp [deriv_cos, deriv_sin]
  have hg''_nonneg : ∀ t, 0 ≤ deriv^[2] g t := by
    intro t
    -- g''(t) = -cos t + 1 = 1 - cos t ≥ 0
    dsimp [g]
    -- Need to compute second derivative and show it's nonnegative
    -- Using calculus: deriv (deriv g) t = 1 - cos t ≥ 0 since cos t ≤ 1
    sorry
  -- g is convex (g'' ≥ 0), so g(t) ≥ g(0) + g'(0)·(t-0) = g(0)
  -- Hence g(x) ≥ 0, which is the desired inequality
  have h_convex : ConvexOn ℝ Set.univ g := by
    refine convexOn_univ_of_deriv2_nonneg (by
      -- g is C²
      refine ContDiff.differentiable ?_ le_rfl
      refine (contDiff_cos.sub ?_).add ?_
      · exact contDiff_const
      · refine (contDiff_const.div_const ?_).mul (contDiff_id.pow 2)
        norm_num
    ) hg''_nonneg
  have h_min : ∀ t, g 0 ≤ g t := by
    intro t
    -- For convex differentiable g, g(t) ≥ g(0) + g'(0)(t-0) = g(0)
    -- since g'(0) = 0
    sorry
  have h := h_min x
  dsimp [g] at h
  linarith
